title: Ensure that clusters are accessible only by using private  endpoints

input_row.title: Ensure that clusters are accessible only by using private  endpoints
input_row.description: Disable the public service endpoint so that communication to the master from both the worker nodes and cluster users is established over the private network through the private service endpoint.
input_row.rationale: When using only the private service endpoint, services are no longer being served on an internet routable IP address. It is more secure and increasingly common for cloud consumers to want limited or no access to the public internet from any of their services.
input_row.audit: From Console: 1. Log in to the IBM Cloud console at https://cloud.ibm.com/. 2. To view a list of your resources, go to Menu > Resource List. 3. From your IBM Cloud resource list, select your cluster. 4. From the Overview tab, if the Public service endpoint URL field has a button that says Enable, then the public service endpoint is disabled. From Command Line: 1. Get the info for your cluster. ibmcloud ks cluster get --cluster 2. If the Public Service Endpoint URL field in the output is not populated, then the public service endpoint is disabled.
input_row.remediation: From Console: 1. Log in to the IBM Cloud console at https://cloud.ibm.com/. 2. To view a list of your resources, go to Menu > Resource List. 3. From your IBM Cloud resource list, select your cluster. 4. From the Overview tab, click the Disable button for the public service endpoint. 5. In the modal, click Disable to confirm. 6. In the next modal, click Refresh to initiate an API server refresh. 7. Optional: Add capacity to your cluster by resizing the worker pool. The pods on the worker node can be rescheduled and continue running on the added worker nodes during the update. 8. From the Worker Nodes tab, select your worker nodes and click Replace. 9. In the modal, click Replace to confirm. From Command Line: 1. Disable the public service endpoint. ibmcloud ks cluster feature disable public-service-endpoint --cluster 2. Confirm the action by clicking yes. 3. Optional: Add capacity to your cluster by resizing the worker pool. The pods on the worker node can be rescheduled and continue running on the added worker nodes during the update. 4. Replace the worker nodes so that their configuration is updated to remove the public service endpoint. ibmcloud ks worker replace --cluster --worker
input_row.impact: To access the master, cluster users must either be in your VPC network or connect through a VPC VPN connection. If your worker nodes must access a public endpoint outside of the cluster, you must enable a public gateway on the VPC subnets that worker nodes are deployed to.
input_row.default_value: The public service endpoint is enabled by default. The private service endpoint is enabled and cannot be disabled.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The audit field explicitly provides clear commands for verification through the IBM Cloud CLI.
- The control's focus on the public service endpoint being disabled is a standard feature directly related to IBM Cloud Kubernetes services, which can be queried through APIs.
- The criteria are met because the public service endpoint status can be verified programmatically using either the CLI command or via API calls to IBM Cloud Kubernetes service.

**PROGRAM NAME:** ibm_kubernetes_cluster_public_service_endpoint_disabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the IBM Cloud CLI command: `ibmcloud ks cluster get --cluster <cluster_name>` to retrieve information about the cluster's configuration.
- Check the output for the "Public Service Endpoint URL" field; it should not be populated (indicating that the public service endpoint is disabled).
- Pass/Fail criteria:
  - **Pass:** If the command outputs that the "Public Service Endpoint URL" field is empty, confirming the endpoint is disabled.
  - **Fail:** If the "Public Service Endpoint URL" is populated or the command returns an error indicating the feature is not disabled.

By leveraging the above details, the automated audit can determine compliance with the specified control effectively.
